page: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU hardware, and it offers zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The CPU cache configuration consists of 32 KB L1d, 0.5 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core scoring in the top 25% at 2924.48. Multi-core performance is average, as shown by a stress-ng multi-core score of 5863.06 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 3743.0. Memory performance represents a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score is poor, falling in the bottom 10%, and cached read-only memory bandwidth is weak at 276.87 GB/sec. Large block uncached read and write bandwidths are average at 44.87 GB/sec and 28.8 GB/sec respectively.

The server offers a qualitatively cost-efficient profile for compute-bound workloads that do not require large memory footprints or high-speed local storage. However, memory-intensive tasks face significant performance tradeoffs due to the low 8.0 GB RAM capacity and weak memory bandwidth. Database operations and static web serving throughput are also weak, with Redis SET operations registering at 4.45 million operations per second. This instance is best suited for lightweight compute tasks, single-threaded processing, and basic software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. It contains 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core and 32 MB of L3 cache, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, scoring in the top 25% on stress-ng, while multi-core performance remains average. However, memory performance is a significant bottleneck, with a Passmark memory score in the bottom 10% and weak cached memory bandwidth. This server provides a cost-efficient option for CPU-heavy, low-memory workloads such as single-threaded processing and lightweight software compilation, but is less effective for memory-intensive applications or high-throughput database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM. It features an AMD EPYC 7B13 CPU, delivering strong single-core but weak memory performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2d-highcpu-4 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 4 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores for single-threaded workloads with minimal memory requirements.
bullet_points: 4 dedicated vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Strong single-core CPU performance ranking in the top 25% of benchmarks
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Significant memory performance limitations with a poor Passmark memory score
Best suited for lightweight compute tasks, single-threaded applications, and software compilation
